evals.prompt: I have a .NET iOS app using ZXing.Net.Mobile for barcode scanning. I want to replace it with SparkScan. Here is my view controller: ZxingViewController.cs
evals.expected_output: Removes all ZXing.Net.Mobile APIs (MobileBarcodeScanner, MobileBarcodeScanningOptions, ZXing.BarcodeFormat). Adds SparkScan integration with matching symbologies (Ean13Upca, Code128, Qr) using new SparkScan(settings) + SparkScanView.Create with this.View as parent. Preserves the ScannedBarcode record and deduplication logic.

assertions.text: ZXing.Mobile / ZXing using directives are NOT present in the new code
MobileBarcodeScanner is NOT instantiated in the new code (may appear in a before/after summary table)
MobileBarcodeScanningOptions is NOT present in the new code
Scandit.DataCapture using directives ARE present
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
Symbology.Qr is enabled (not Symbology.QrCode, which does not exist)
Symbology.QrCode does NOT appear as a Symbology enum value (BarcodeFormat.QR_CODE in a summary table is acceptable)
new SparkScan(settings) is used (not SparkScan.Create or BarcodeCapture.Create)
SparkScanView.Create( is called with this.View (or this.View!) as the parentView argument (NOT a SparkScanCoordinatorLayout, which is Android-only)
Either sparkScan.BarcodeScanned += handler is wired, or ISparkScanListener is implemented
ViewWillAppear forwards to sparkScanView.PrepareScanning()
ViewWillDisappear forwards to sparkScanView.StopScanning()
If args.FrameData / ImageBuffers are accessed, they are wrapped in `using var` (or explicitly disposed)
ScannedBarcode record is preserved in the output
scannedBarcodes list is preserved in the output
Deduplication logic (checking for existing value before adding) is preserved
A summary of changes is shown with what was removed and what was added
Setup checklist mentions Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode as a NuGet package
Setup checklist mentions Scandit.DataCapture.Core as a NuGet package
A concrete version number is used for the dependencies (not a [version] placeholder)
Setup checklist mentions NSCameraUsageDescription
Setup checklist mentions calling ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() and ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize() in AppDelegate.FinishedLaunching (required for SDK 8.0+)
